title: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4
content:
{{Top|rwm|old|title=''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4}} {{ComicBook |image=[[File:TalesOfTheJedi-TheGoldenAgeOfTheSith4.jpg]] |title=''Tales of the Jedi –
The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4 |writer=[[Kevin J. Anderson]] |penciller=[[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] |inker=*[[Mark G. Heike]] *[[Bill Black]] *[[David Jacob Beckett]] |letterer=[[Willie Schubert]] |colorist=[[Perry McNamee]] |cover artist=[[Russell Walks]] |editor=[[Bob Cooper]] |publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]] |release date=[[January 8]], [[1997]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54593/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_golden_age_of_the_sith_1996_4|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith (1996) #4}} {{C|First printing}} |pages=26 |upc=761568954166 |timeline=[[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |series=''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]'' |issue=4 }} '''''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4''' is the fourth, penultimate issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] miniseries ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith]]''. It was written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]], illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]], and first published on [[January 8]], [[1997]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]]. The issue features the story "'''Pawns of a Sith Lord'''," in which [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] begins to initiate [[Gav Daragon]] in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. ==Publisher's summary== ''When Gav and Jori Daragon were first captured by the evil Sith, they feared for their lives. Now, Jori fears for her brother's ''spirit'', as would-be Dark Lord Naga Sadow begins to initiate him into the ways of the dark side of the Force. Naga Sadow is playing a deadly game of chess, and the Daragon siblings are his pawns.'' ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=Pawns of a Sith Lord |text=Fleeing from bad decisions in the Empress Teta system, young hyperspace explorers Gav and Jori Daragon fly off into the unknown. Their ship, ''Starbreaker 12'', chooses a random course — a fateful route that takes them to the fabled Sith Empire, where they are captured and held prisoner. Unknowingly, Gav and Jori have landed in the middle of a bitter power struggle between two of the greatest Sith Lords — Naga Sadow and Ludo Kressh. In the Sith council chambers, the Lords argue over what is to be done with the captives. Kressh fears that the ''Starbreaker 12'' is the precursor to an invasion, while Sadow sees the two trespassers as keys to a new section of the galaxy ripe for Sith conquest. He feels that if he can unite the squabbling factions against a common enemy, a common goal, then the Sith Empire can rise to new heights of glory. Gav and Jori wait in their cell, sentenced to be executed, despairing…not realizing that they have become pawns of a Sith Lord. With his fierce and loyal Massassi warriors, Naga Sadow secretly steals strange Republic weaponry from the impounded ''Starbreaker 12'' and stages a commando raid to free the prisoners, killing a prominent Sith Lord in the process. Sadow whisks Gav and Jori off to his private fortress on the moon of Khar Shian… The Sith Lords are thrown into an uproar, seeing the weapons, the evidence, and assuming they have been attacked by Republic forces. In their turmoil, they turn to Naga Sadow for leadership. Outraged, Ludo Kressh storms off — but Sadow has achieved his greatest desire: he is chosen as the next Dark Lord of the Sith…}} ==Plot summary== ===A new Dark Lord=== On [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]], the new [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] receives a mark on his forehead from two [[Sith pincerbug]]s. Meanwhile, [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]] goes with [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] and [[Memit Nadill]] to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to address the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] on Odan-Urr's recent vision. The Senate seems completely unconvinced, but Teta vows to at least strengthen the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system's]] army. ===Sadow's influence=== Elsewhere, at Naga Sadow's [[Naga Sadow's Khar Shian citadel|Khar Shian citadel]], the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gav Daragon]] uses a Sith amulet to conjure up illusions. Sadow compliments Daragon, telling the young [[Human/Legends|Human]] that he has potential and that the Sith magic that he learns will be a powerful ally in their [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|coming struggle]]. He tells Gav to learn to become adept with Sith magic if he wants to get home safely. Sadow puts the next stage of his plan into action by sending his loyal [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors to infiltrate Ziost and steal the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]''. The Dark Lord then travels to his [[Naga Sadow's Khar Delba citadel|decoy fortress]] on the nearby planet of [[Khar Delba]] where [[Jori Daragon]] is being held. When Jori asks when she can see her brother, Sadow claims he is protecting them since they are both still under the execution order. When Jori tells Sadow that she needs her ship back, he reassures her that he has already made some "extreme arrangements." ===Kressh's retribution=== On Ziost, Sadow's Massassi warriors infiltrate the ''Starbreaker 12''{{'s}} impound hangar and kill [[Ludo Kressh]]'s guards. Before escaping on the ''Starbreaker 12'', they purposely leave behind behind Naga Sadow's emblem, which Ludo Kressh soon discovers. Realizing that Sadow had staged a false flag attack in order to seize power, Kressh calls together his fellow Sith Lords [[Horak-mul]] and [[Dor Gal-ram]], demanding an attack on Sadow's fortress. Several [[Standard day/Legends|days]] later, Sadow returns to Jori on Khar Delba. He brings a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] message from her brother Gav, who tells her that Sadow has taught him to use his [[The Force/Legends|Force powers]] in a "different way" and that Sadow had made arrangements for them to return to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Sadow then shows Jori the ''Starbreaker 12'', which he has recovered. He tells her that she is free to go home and to escape from this nightmare. Before Jori can leave, Kressh's fleet, led by Horak-mul and Gal-ram, attacks the icy world. Jori panics and says they have to get to cover. Sadow, however, is not worried, as this is all part of his plan. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4}} ===Editions=== *UPC 761568954166; [[January 8]], [[1997]]; [[Dark Horse Comics]]; First printing *{{C|No UPC}}; [[November 10]], [[2015]]; [[Marvel Comics]]; Digital
